---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
  \usepackage{tikz}
  \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, positioning, shapes.geometric}
  \usetikzlibrary{overlay-beamer-styles}
---

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDE
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{7.2 - Collecting normative data}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```

## Who will be part normative data?

People that **we assume** are normal,
\pause

Often, people without acknowledged problems. E.g., people without neurological or psychiatric disease.

::: {.callout-important}
The composition of normative data will influence the **inferences** can be drawn from the comparison.
:::


## Collecting normative data
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}

% --- Your figure -------------------------------------------------------
\node (img) at (0,0) {\includegraphics[width=0.7\textwidth]{Figures/normative_data.png}};

% Top-left
\only<1>{\fill[white] (-3.5,3) rectangle (0,0);}

% Top-right
\only<1-2>{\fill[white] (-0.2,3) rectangle (3.2,0);}

% Bottom-left
\only<1-3>{\fill[white] (-3.5,0) rectangle (0,-3);}

% Bottom-right
\only<1-4>{\fill[white] (-0.2,0) rectangle (3.2,-3);}


\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
\onslide<5> % <-- forces slide 5 to exist
The vertical line is one potential \textbf{threshold}.

\pause
```

## From continuous variables to "categories"

The use of thresholds is a way that helps us to move from a continuous variable, to **categories** with important pragmatic values, but with potential problems.
